title: Anchor All-Hands Q3 2026
compiled_truth:
The Q3 2026 all-hands was held on October 1st, 2026 — the first full-company meeting since Anchor crossed the 8-person mark. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened with a recap of the quarter: two design partners onboarded, the single-pass primitive benchmarks finalized, and the initial packaging of the SDK for external consumption. Revenue remains pre-commercial, but Seren framed Q3 as the quarter where "we stopped building for ourselves and started building for customers." She was unusually direct about burn rate — roughly 14 months of runway remaining — and urged the team to think in terms of proof points that accelerate the next raise.

[Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) walked through engineering OKRs. The core pipeline hit its latency targets on three of four workloads; the outlier was high-entropy streaming, which [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) attributed to a scheduler regression introduced in late August. Miro gave a brief post-mortem on the regression and confirmed a fix was already in staging. Dani emphasized that Q4 engineering priority is stability over new features — "no heroics, just test coverage."

[Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) presented the go-to-market update. Two design partners — one in logistics, one in media processing — are running integration pilots. Feedback has been positive on throughput but both partners flagged documentation gaps, especially around error handling semantics. [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) took an action item to overhaul the developer docs by end of October.

[Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) shared early hiring plans for Q4: one additional infrastructure engineer and a developer relations hire. [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) raised the question of whether devrel was premature given the product's maturity, which sparked a longer discussion. Seren sided with Paz, arguing that community building needed to start before GA. [Kael Petrov](people/kael-petrov) mostly listened but contributed a useful point about aligning devrel content with the SDK stabilization schedule so messaging doesn't outpace the product.

The meeting closed with a brief roadmap walkthrough. GA target remains Q1 2027. Seren asked everyone to flag blockers asynchrounously in the shared tracker rather than waiting for the next all-hands. Overall tone was cautiously optimistic — the team knows the next two quarters are make-or-break for [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor).
timeline: - **2026-08-22** | High-entropy streaming scheduler regression introduced; identified by [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) two weeks later.
- **2026-09-10** | Second design partner (media processing vertical) begins integration pilot with Anchor SDK.
- **2026-09-25** | Pre-read circulated by [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) outlining Q3 metrics and Q4 priorities ahead of all-hands.
- **2026-10-01** | Q3 all-hands held; full team of 8 in attendance. Roadmap reaffirmed GA target of Q1 2027.
- **2026-10-31** | Developer documentation overhaul completed by [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah), addressing partner feedback on error handling semantics.
- **2026-11-15** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) opens req for developer relations hire; first round of interviews scheduled for December.
- **2026-12-09** | Scheduler regression fix shipped to production; high-entropy streaming workload hits latency targets.
- **2027-01-20** | Follow-up all-hands planned to assess GA readiness and review Q4 OKRs.

_facts.name: Anchor All-Hands Q3 2026
